title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: Wildlife conservation has (0) increasingly important as human activities continue to threaten natural habitats. Many species are now (1) the verge of extinction due to deforestation, pollution, and climate change. Conservation organisations are working tirelessly to (2) awareness about these issues and protect endangered animals. National parks and nature reserves have been (3) up around the world to provide safe havens for wildlife. Scientists are also (4) out research to better understand animal behaviour and develop effective protection strategies. Breeding programmes have helped to (5) back several species from the brink of extinction. However, conservation efforts often (6) up against challenges such as limited funding and political obstacles. Local communities play a crucial role in wildlife protection, as they can (7) after the natural areas near their homes. Education is essential to ensure that future generations (8) responsibility for preserving the planet's biodiversity for years to come.

example.explanation: 'Become' is the correct verb to indicate a change of state.

questions.explanation: 'On the verge of' (with 'the verge of' already in the text) is the fixed expression for being on the verge of something.
'Raise awareness' is the correct placement to raise awareness.
'Set up' (with 'up' already in the text) means to establish or create something new.
'Carrying out research' (with 'out' already in the text) is the correct collocation for carrying out research.
'Bring back' (with 'back' already in the text) means to bring something back into existence or flourish.
'Come up against' (with 'up against' already in the text) means to face problems or obstacles.
'Look after' (with 'after' already in the text) is the most common phrasal verb for taking care of something.
'Take responsibility for' is the fixed expression for assuming responsibility.
